steps.title: Identify the structural contrast
steps.content:
The first four lines describe human successes at controlling nature: taming forests, irrigating deserts, making them “blossom at command.” Then the poem shifts with “We must always leave the borders of the sea,” emphasizing the sea's immeasurable vastness.

The contrast is between what we can control and what we cannot.

steps.title: Apply the contrast to the choices
steps.content:
D captures this: the poem presents attempts to control nature and then offers a reminder that not all nature is controllable.
  • A misreads the first part as arguing against interference
  • B mistakes the dynamic for temporality
  • C misidentifies the second part as challenging the approach when it's actually acknowledging its limits
.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
